title: Executive Order 12544—Blocking Libyan Government Property in the United States or Held by U.S. Persons

flags.justification: The Secretary of the Treasury is authorized to employ 'all powers granted' by IEEPA — a broad grant, though of a standard, well-established kind for asset-blocking sanctions orders.
No review process for affected parties is specified in this short order's own text.
Single coherent purpose: blocking Libyan government property.
'All property and interests in property' is broad, standard sanctions-order language, with specifics left to be elaborated in subsequent implementing regulations rather than defined here.
No incentive structure rewarding behavior contrary to the order's purpose is identifiable.
No review or reauthorization mechanism is specified in this order's own text, despite being tied to a resolvable underlying threat.
The order is explicitly required to be transmitted to Congress and published in the Federal Register — a real transparency requirement.
No external check on the Secretary's exercise of blocking authority is specified in this order's own text.
A companion and expansion order to an already-established sanctions framework (EO 12543), not a novel authority claim on its own.
No exemptions are created by this order.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 5/18 = 27.8%. A companion order to EO 12543, part of the Reagan administration's 1986 response to Libya-linked terrorism.
